method_atom: high-velocity scattering of a self-focusing nonlinear wave packet off a localized attractive point defect: a cubic-nonlinear dispersive evolution equation with a delta-function potential well is analyzed in the fast-incidence limit, yielding explicit asymptotic formulas that split the incoming coherent pulse into transmitted and reflected coherent components plus a small dispersive/radiative remainder, and showing that the fraction captured by the well's bound state vanishes as velocity grows.
object_atom: a matter-wave soliton governed by the Gross-Pitaevskii equation scattering off an attractive delta-function impurity potential.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is the perturbative fast-limit splitting of a coherent nonlinear wave by a localized attractive potential with vanishing trapping; the domain-specific object is the Gross-Pitaevskii matter-wave soliton and its delta impurity.
